title: Lightly Spiced Tunisian Couscous Pilaf
description: A spicy, light, and nutritious Tunisian dish featuring couscous, with an al-dente finish, friendly to Ayurvedic Rajasic dietary preferences. This meal-prep recipe is perfect for a quick and healthy lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and sauté for 5 minutes until translucent.
Add 1 cup of water and bring to a boil.
Stir in 1 cup of couscous, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the couscous is al dente.
Stir in a pinch of cumin, coriander, and paprika for extra flavor.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze for future meals, up to 3 days or 2 months, respectively.
To reheat, simply microwave or pan-fry with a splash of water until warmed through.
Garnish with additional parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Enjoy your delicious and healthy Lightly Spiced Tunisian Couscous Pilaf!
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for future meals.
Reheat as needed, following the same instructions.
